mnemonics: Pandas Excel-s: Think of pandas (the animal) using Excel spreadsheets as their favorite snack. This reminds you that the pandas library is used to read and write Excel files in Python.
keyPoints: Use the pandas.read_excel() function to read data from an Excel file into a DataFrame.
Use the DataFrame.to_excel() method to write data from a DataFrame to an Excel file.
Specify the sheet_name parameter to read from or write to a specific worksheet within an Excel workbook.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to install the required 'openpyxl' or 'xlrd' engine (e.g., `pip install openpyxl`) to read/write .xlsx files, which causes a ModuleNotFoundError.
Not specifying the full file path or using incorrect slashes (\\ on Windows, / on Mac/Linux), leading to a FileNotFoundError.
Assuming the first row is always the header; always check the header parameter in read_excel() if your data doesn't have one or has a header in a different row.
